rulings.Case Title: FRANKLIN ESCOBAR VS. GINA HUGHES ET AL
rulings.Calendar Matter: Notice Of Motion And Motion For Defendants Motion To Compel Responses To Deposition Questions
rulings.Rulings: Real Property/Housing Court Law and Motion Calendar for Monday, July 16, 2018, Line 1. DEFENDANT GINA HUGHES' Motion To Compel Responses To Deposition Questions. OFF CALENDAR, per request of the moving party. =(501/REQ)

rulings.Case Title: BINGSEN LI VS. OCWEN LOAN SERVICING ET AL
rulings.Rulings: Real Property/Housing Court Law and Motion Calendar for Monday, July 16, 2018, Line 2. DEFENDANT OCWEN LOAN SERVICING'S DEMURRER TO COMPLAINT. Sustained with leave to amend as to all causes of action asserted against the moving Defendant. Plaintiff is to allege in good faith facts in support of each element of each cause of action, inducing but not limited to: (1) basis for Ocwen's liability as to the origination of the operative loan and DOT; (2) basis for Ocwen's duty as a lender or a successive beneficiary to investigate chain of title for forgeries; (3) facts supporting delayed discovery in light of constructive notice Bank of New York Mellon v. Citibank (2017) 8 Cal.App.5th 935; (4) facts supporting Ocwen's present adverse claim to title; (5) facts supporting Plaintiff's claim to hold 100% of the property in constructive trust. =(501/REQ)

rulings.Case Title: THE JOHN STEWART COMPANY VS. EUGINA KRIGER ET AL
rulings.Rulings: Real Property/Housing Court Law and Motion Calendar for Monday, July 16, 2018, Line 3. DEFENDANT EUGENIA KRIGER'S MOTION FOR SUMMARY JUDGMENT. DENIED. Moving party failed to shift its burden. The Court notes that contrary to Defendant's misleading assertion in the Points and Authorities that defendant was allowed to maintain possession ("¿ a neutral officer issued an order allowing Defendant to remain in her home. Plaintiff elected to file this lawsuit in spite of the order". Points and Authorities 1:23-24), the hearing officer's determination was conditional and "without waiving, changing, or cancelling the 14 Day Notice". Defendant omits the conditions from its discussion. Consequently, no argument and no evidence is presented that, for example, Plaintiff failed to enter into the Settlement Agreement as ordered; or Defendant was ready and willing to enter into the Settlement Agreement as ordered; or that Defendant complied with the conditions. =(501/REQ)

rulings.Case Title: THE JOHN STEWART COMPANY VS. EUGINA KRIGER ET AL
rulings.Rulings: Real Property/Housing Court Law and Motion Calendar for Monday, July 16, 2018, Line 3. PLAINTIFF THE JOHN STEWART COMPANY'S MOTION FOR SUMMARY JUDGMENT. DENIED. Triable issue of fact regarding compliance with Informal Hearing Decision. See Kriger Declaration paragraphs 10-12. =(501/REQ)

rulings.Case Title: MOHAMED A. KARAH VS. KWANASIA SMITH
rulings.Calendar Matter: Notice Of Motion And Motion (1) To Compel Discovery Responses Without Objections; (2) To Deem Facts Admitted Not Responded To (3) To Continue Trial Date To July 23, 2018; And (4) For Sanction
rulings.Rulings: Real Property/Housing Court Law and Motion Calendar for Monday, July 16, 2018, Line 4. DEFENDANT KWANASIA SMITH'S Motion (1) To Compel Discovery Responses Without Objections; (2) To Deem Facts Admitted Not Responded To (3) To Continue Trial Date To July 23, 2018; And (4) For Sanctions. GRANTED, subject to opposition. Admissions are deemed admitted. Defendant to provide complete verified responses without objections to Request for production and Special Interrogatories by hand-delivery by no later than July 18, 2018. Trial is continued to July 23, 2018. Sanctions granted in the amount of $550. =(501/REQ)

rulings.Case Title: SECURITY PUBLIC STORAGE-SAN FRANCISCO VS. CALEBE O LIMA
rulings.Rulings: Real Property/Housing Court Law and Motion Calendar for Monday, July 16, 2018, Line 5. DEFENDANT CALEBE LIMA'S DEMURRER TO COMPLAINT. OVERRULED, 5 days to answer. Complaint sufficiently states a cause of action for unlawful detainer and is not uncertain. =(501/REQ)
